qa.overall_assessment: BA spec is largely solid — evidence is grounded, scope is tight, codex findings were incorporated correctly. However two blocking objections prevent approval: (1) AC11 is labeled as a 'primary false-positive fix' case but the current hook does NOT block that command — it already passes without the fix, making it a forward-proofing test misclassified as a false-positive remediation; (2) the line-743 two-condition ambiguity (which grep gets COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED vs which stays on raw COMMAND) lives only in Technical Hints and is absent from the Must Have requirements, creating a real risk that dev applies the stripped view to the path-match condition and silently weakens the workflow-file protection. One additional minor objection covers AC13's fictional test binary. The spec is close to approval; the two blocking items require targeted BA revision.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: context.json states hook is 1051 lines; actual file wc -l = 1050 lines. Likely off-by-one from missing trailing newline. Non-blocking discrepancy.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec invocation was itself BLOCKED by the hook at line 715 — the COMMAND variable contained 'killall' and 'claude' in the prompt text, triggering the exact false-positive this spec is meant to fix. This is direct live confirmation of the bug. Self-review substituted per graceful fallback protocol.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted; 5+ adversarial questions generated and addressed internally (AC label accuracy, compound-condition ambiguity, fictional binary testability, AC14 pre-fix pass status, algorithm coverage for all 16 ACs)

ba_validation_objections.title: AC11 labeled 'primary false-positive fix' but current hook already passes it
ba_validation_objections.finding: AC11 uses command 'python3 -c \'import os; # rm is not needed here\'' and is classified in the spec as a 'primary false-positive fix' case (exit 0 expected after fix). QA verified by Python regex simulation against the full compound conditions of lines 715, 722, 743, and 751 that this command does NOT trigger the current hook before the fix. Line 743 requires BOTH '(rm|mv)\s' AND a workflow-path match — the workflow path is absent. Line 751 requires '(^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s' — the semicolon in '; # rm' is followed by ' # rm', where '#' intervenes between ';' and 'rm', breaking the pattern. Lines 715 and 722 have no match at all. Therefore AC11 is a mislabeled forward-proofing test, not a remediation of a current false positive. The spec's C1 'Could Have' requirement (strip Python comments) addresses a future concern, not an existing bug. Misclassifying a passing case as a false-positive fix misleads dev about which cases NEED the fix to pass, and may cause dev to implement C1 as a Must Have rather than a Could Have.
ba_validation_objections.evidence: Python simulation output: 'PASSES AC11 (already passes without fix)' using full compound condition checks matching hook source at lines 743/751. Verified by reading hook source lines 743 and 751 directly.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA must: (1) Remove the 'primary false-positive fix' label from AC11. (2) Reclassify AC11 as a 'future-proofing' or 'C1 coverage' test case — it verifies that C1 (Could Have) functionality works, not that a current bug is fixed. (3) Update the THEN clause to clarify: 'exit code is 0 (allowed) — if C1 is implemented, Python comment text is stripped before matching; if C1 is not implemented, this case already passes under the current hook'. (4) Add a TRUE current false-positive case for C1: e.g. 'python3 -c \'print("rm")\'' where rm appears in a non-comment Python string literal — but confirm this actually triggers the hook before adding it as a FP case.
ba_validation_objections.verification_command: python3 /tmp/qa-ac11-verify2.py (output: PASSES AC11 (already passes without fix))

ba_validation_objections.title: Line 743 two-condition partial-strip ambiguity absent from Must Have requirements
ba_validation_objections.finding: Line 743 in the hook has TWO grep conditions joined with '&&': (1) 'echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE \'(rm|mv)\s\'' and (2) 'echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE \'(workflow-[^/]*.json|\.claude/todos/)\''. The spec's M2 says 'Apply the four affected rules (lines 715, 722, 743, 751) against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED instead of raw $COMMAND.' This is ambiguous for line 743: should BOTH conditions use COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED, or only the first? The Technical Hints section (not a requirement) says 'Line 743: change first grep only — the workflow path grep on line 743b can remain on raw COMMAND since it is a path match, not a dangerous-token match.' This critical distinction exists ONLY in the Technical Hints section, not in M2 or any Must Have requirement. A dev who reads M2 but not Technical Hints will apply COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED to BOTH greps on line 743 — which would be wrong: stripping non-executable contexts from the workflow-path check could allow a command like 'echo "mv .claude/todos/x.json elsewhere"' to bypass the workflow file protection if the path is inside a quoted echo argument.
ba_validation_objections.evidence: Read hook source line 743: 'if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE \'(rm|mv)\s\' && echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE \'(workflow-[^/]*.json|\.claude/todos/)\''. Technical Hints in ticket specifies line 743b stays on raw COMMAND. M2 requirement text: 'Apply the four affected rules (lines 715, 722, 743, 751) against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED' — does not distinguish sub-conditions.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA must update M2 to be explicit about line 743's split treatment. Revised M2 text: 'Apply the four affected rules against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED instead of raw $COMMAND — with one exception: line 743 has two grep conditions; only the first condition (the dangerous-token check: (rm|mv)\s) is applied against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the second condition (the workflow-path check: workflow-[^/]*.json|\.claude/todos/) MUST remain on raw $COMMAND to preserve workflow-file protection.' Alternatively, extract this constraint as a new Must Have requirement M11: 'For the compound line-743 rule, only the dangerous-token grep is applied against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the path-match grep remains on raw $COMMAND.'
ba_validation_objections.verification_command: Read hook source line 743; cross-reference M2 text in ticket vs Technical Hints section

ba_validation_objections.title: AC13 uses fictional binary 'some-doc-tool' instead of the actual false-positive command
ba_validation_objections.finding: The real false-positive that motivated this fix was 'codex exec "...killall...claude..."' (confirmed: BA observed the block during the BA session when codex exec was called with a prompt containing 'killall' near 'claude'). AC13's test command uses 'some-doc-tool "document that killall and claude are monitored"' — a fictional binary. The hook does not check whether a binary exists (it just greps the raw string), so the fictional binary IS technically testable. However, the spec's description in the GIVEN clause ('e.g. a Skill invocation or documentation command with a prompt body') and the fictional binary name create documentation drift from the real FP case. Dev writing the regression test will have to guess what realistic non-shell binary to use, and the test may not exercise the actual code path that caused the original production block.
ba_validation_objections.evidence: User requirement document: 'codex exec ... killall ... claude' blocked by line 715 (observed in BA session). Acceptance criteria JSON command field: 'some-doc-tool "document that killall and claude are monitored"'. Pattern match confirmed: (killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker) matches both the fictional and real command.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: Replace fictional binary with the actual false-positive command. Update AC13 check.command to: 'codex exec "The hook documentation says killall and claude processes are protected"'. Update the GIVEN clause: 'The hook processes the command that originally caused the block: codex exec with a prompt body containing killall and claude as documentation tokens'. This makes the regression test directly trace to the original incident.
ba_validation_objections.note: This is minor — the fictional command IS testable and does exercise the right code path. Approval is not blocked by this finding alone.

evidence_quality.summary: BA read the actual hook file and identified the exact lines/patterns causing false positives. Live block observed during BA session (codex exec blocked by line 715). Code snippets in pre_existing_guards match source. One minor issue: AC13 uses fictional binary instead of the actual codex exec command that was blocked. Line count discrepancy (1051 vs 1050) is trivial.

scope_alignment.summary: Scope is tight: only hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py (new), hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py (new). Won't Have explicitly excludes full AST parsing, all other hooks, and Layer 1/absolute-ban rules. No over-engineering detected. User requirement said 'layered heuristics only' and the spec delivers exactly that.

investigation_completeness.summary: BA investigated which specific rules cause false positives (lines 715, 722, 743, 751 in the general safety section vs Layer 1.A-E which are intentionally broad). All 4 codex BLOCKER findings and all 5 MAJOR findings are accounted for in the spec with explicit disposition records. Live reproduction confirmed during BA session.

affected_file_accuracy.summary: File hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh exists and contains the patterns BA claims at the lines BA claims. Line 715 pattern '(killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)' verified. Line 722 pattern 'kill\s+-' verified. Line 743 compound condition verified. Line 751 pattern '(^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s' verified. hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py confirmed absent (new file). hooks/tests/ exists with other test files — new file can be placed there.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.summary: Two blocking drifts found: (1) AC11 is labeled as a false-positive fix case but the current hook already passes it — the label 'primary false-positive fix' is factually incorrect and could drive wrong implementation priorities. (2) M2 requirement text is ambiguous about line 743's two-condition split — Technical Hints carries critical information that belongs in the binding requirements. Both require BA revision before dev proceeds.

success_criteria_results.criterion: AC commands can be run against the hook to produce the expected exit codes
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Pattern simulation for all MUST-NOT-BLOCK cases; check MUST-BLOCK cases against current patterns
success_criteria_results.details: BLOCKING OBJECTIONS: (1) AC11 command already passes the current hook (exits 0 without fix) — labeled as 'primary false-positive fix' case but is actually forward-proofing. (2) M2 requirement is ambiguous about line 743 two-condition partial-strip — Technical Hints carries binding information that belongs in requirements. NON-BLOCKING: AC13 uses fictional binary 'some-doc-tool' instead of 'codex exec'. AC14 already passes (SIGKILL does not match kill\s+-) — correctly classified as regression guard.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Python simulation: AC11 PASSES before fix; AC14 PASSES before fix; AC10/AC13 BLOCK before fix (confirmed false positives)

all_findings.issue: AC11 labeled as 'primary false-positive fix' case but current hook already passes it. The command 'python3 -c \'import os; # rm is not needed here\'' does not trigger line 715, 722, 743 (compound condition fails — no workflow path), or 751 (semicolon pattern broken by '#' between ';' and 'rm'). Misclassifying a passing case as a false-positive fix case distorts developer priorities and may cause C1 to be treated as Must Have.
all_findings.recommendation: Reclassify AC11 as a C1 (Could Have) forward-proofing test. Update THEN clause to note it already passes without C1. If BA wants a true false-positive case for Python comment stripping, find a command that actually triggers the current hook.

all_findings.issue: M2 requirement says 'apply the four affected rules against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED' without distinguishing that line 743's second grep (path match) must stay on raw COMMAND. This ambiguity risks dev applying COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED to the workflow-path check, potentially weakening protection against 'rm/mv .claude/todos/' commands when the path appears inside a quoted argument.
all_findings.recommendation: Update M2 to explicitly split line 743's treatment: only the dangerous-token condition uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the path-match condition remains on raw COMMAND. Or add a new M11 requirement stating this constraint.

all_findings.issue: AC13 uses fictional binary 'some-doc-tool' instead of the real false-positive command 'codex exec "...killall...claude..."'. This is the actual command that was blocked in the BA session and in cycle 20260519-211515. The fictional binary is testable (hook greps raw string), but the test does not directly trace to the incident.
all_findings.recommendation: Replace 'some-doc-tool "document that killall and claude are monitored"' with 'codex exec "The hook documentation says killall and claude processes are protected"'. Update GIVEN clause to reference the original incident command.

failures.issue: AC11 mislabeled as primary false-positive fix — current hook already passes this command without fix
failures.recommendation: Reclassify as forward-proofing test for C1 (Could Have). Find a command that actually triggers the current hook if a true Python FP case is needed.

failures.issue: M2 requirement ambiguous about line 743 two-condition partial-strip — binding constraint lives only in Technical Hints
failures.recommendation: Elevate line 743 split treatment to Must Have requirement or update M2 text explicitly

summary.release_recommendation: reject — BA must revise AC11 classification and M2 requirement before dev proceeds

failed_criteria: spec_text_vs_execution_drift: AC11 labeled false-positive fix but current hook passes it
spec_text_vs_execution_drift: M2 requirement ambiguous about line 743 two-condition partial-strip
major_issues: AC11 reclassification: change from 'primary false-positive fix' to 'C1 forward-proofing test' — update THEN clause to note it already passes before fix
M2 clarification: add explicit constraint that line 743's path-match condition stays on raw $COMMAND; only the dangerous-token condition uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA makes two targeted edits: (1) AC11 in both ticket and acceptance-criteria JSON — relabel and update THEN clause. (2) M2 in ticket — add the line-743 split-condition constraint in the requirement text or add M11. No structural rework needed. All other dimensions pass.
refined_context.additional_context: QA independently reproduced the false-positive during this session (codex exec blocked by line 715 when prompt body contained 'killall' near 'claude'). This confirms BA's core evidence. The two blocking issues are precision defects in the spec, not fundamental analysis errors.
